user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Migrate the TC387 3-phase PWM from complementary IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl to three single-ended PWM channels using IfxGtm_Pwm on TOM1, sharing TOM1 CH0 timebase, at 20 kHz center-aligned.
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate three single-ended PWM outputs U/V/W on TOM1 CH2/CH4/CH6, initially at 25%/50%/75% duty. Every 500 ms, step each duty by +10 percentage points with wrap in 0–100%. No deadtime or min-pulse constraints. Synchronous (shadow) update at period match with shared timebase (TOM1 CH0).
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use unified IfxGtm_Pwm to configure a multi-channel synchronized PWM group on GTM TOM1 with center-aligned mode and 20 kHz frequency. Map pins via IfxGtm_PinMap_setTomTout to P00.3 (TOM1 CH2), P00.5 (TOM1 CH4), P00.7 (TOM1 CH6). Enable GTM and required clocks. Start outputs at requested duties using IfxGtm_Pwm_startSyncedChannels. Use IfxStm_Timer to generate a 500 ms tick that calls IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDuty to queue shadow updates applied on the next period match.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified PWM driver supports multi-channel synchronization, center-aligned operation, and manages TOM submodule selection internally. It is available for TC3xx in the template and is ideal for migrating from complementary PwmHl to single-ended channels sharing a timebase.

runtime_function.name: GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_stepDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Every 500 ms, step U/V/W duty by +10% with wrap in 0–100%, applying changes synchronously on period match.

migration_notes.context_preserved: The new configuration keeps TOM1 as the PWM engine with CH0 as the shared timebase and outputs on TOM1 CH2/CH4/CH6. Frequency is 20 kHz, center-aligned, and duties begin at 25/50/75%. Updates occur every 500 ms and are applied synchronously at the next period match using IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDuty. Deadtime and min-pulse constraints are not used, and former low-side pins remain unused. Pin mapping uses IfxGtm_PinMap_setTomTout on the TC38x LFBGA516 package as required.
